$schema: Ground truth for fallow duplication detection benchmark
description: Each clone pair documents a known duplication relationship between two file regions. 'type' follows the standard clone taxonomy (Type-1 through Type-4). 'min_overlap_lines' is the minimum number of shared lines a correct detection should cover.

clone_pairs.description: Exact copy of DataProcessor class with all methods
HTTP client with renamed classes, methods, variables, and interface names. Same structure and logic, different identifiers.
Event bus / message broker with renamed types and methods. Identical structure.
Form validator with added rule types (email, range), modified error format (added severity, timestamp), added logging. Core validate/checkRule logic is shared.
clone_pairs.notes: Strict/mild should find partial overlaps in the shared switch cases

clone_pairs.description: Priority queue with added methods (requeue, peekItem, getProcessedCount), added retryCount field. Core enqueue/binary-search and updatePriority logic is shared.
clone_pairs.notes: Strict/mild should find the binary search insertion and updatePriority blocks

clone_pairs.description: Quicksort vs mergesort — same semantics, completely different implementation. Should NOT be detected by token-based tools.
clone_pairs.notes: Type-4 clones require semantic analysis beyond token matching. Token-based tools should NOT detect these. A detection here is a false positive unless it finds the binary search helper (which has legitimate Type-2 similarity).

negative_pairs.description: Auth service vs LRU cache — both use Maps and have similar CRUD patterns but are semantically distinct
File watcher vs data processor — both extend EventEmitter with start/stop but different domains
Connection pool vs priority queue — both manage ordered items with add/remove but different semantics
Auth service vs file watcher — completely different domains
Connection pool vs HTTP client — both do async request handling but different semantics
